sections.paragraph:
pr. to be loosed, free, opp. to that which is bound, restrained. Hence-

(1) to spread out loose things on the ground (see חֹפֶשׁ ). Arabic خفش II. to stretch out.

(2) to be prostrate, hence to be weak, infirm, as if with one’s strength loosened. (Compare חָלַשׁ. Arabic خفش Med. E.). Hence חָפְשִׁי, חָפְשׁוּת.

(3) to set free, to liberate (a slave). Arab. حفش to be poured out freely.

Pual, to be set free, spoken of a slave, Lev 19:20. Hence the following words-
